title: What is DAR Hypotheken?
content: DAR Hypotheken is the first halal mortgage provider in the Netherlands. DAR offers an interest-free alternative to traditional mortgages, based on the principle of co-ownership. The name DAR means 'home' in Arabic, reflecting the company's core mission: making halal home financing accessible to everyone in the Netherlands. DAR Hypotheken was founded to provide fair and transparent home financing that is fully aligned with Islamic finance principles. The company is based in Amsterdam and operates under the supervision of the Dutch Financial Markets Authority (AFM). DAR Hypotheken makes it possible to buy a home without paying interest, making it a unique product on the Dutch mortgage market.

title: How does co-ownership work?
content: The DAR Mortgage works on the principle of co-ownership (also known as Diminishing Musharakah). This means that DAR Hypotheken and the customer become joint owners of the property. At the start, DAR purchases a portion of the property and the customer purchases the other portion. The customer then gradually buys out DAR's share through monthly payments. As the customer pays off more, their ownership share grows while DAR's share decreases. After full repayment, the customer is the sole and complete owner of the property. This model is fundamentally different from a traditional mortgage because there is no loan with interest, but rather a joint investment in real estate.

title: Fixed monthly payments
content: With the DAR Mortgage, the customer pays a fixed monthly amount consisting of two components: the repayment and the usage fee. The repayment is the amount by which the customer gradually takes over DAR's ownership share. The usage fee is a compensation the customer pays for using the part of the property that is still owned by DAR. This is explicitly not interest, but a fee for the use of the property. The monthly payments are transparent and predictable, so the customer knows exactly what the costs are. The total monthly amount is comparable to what you would pay with a traditional mortgage, but without interest.

title: 30-year repayment plan
content: The DAR Mortgage has a standard term of 30 years. During this period, the customer gradually purchases the full ownership of the property from DAR. The 30-year term is comparable to a standard mortgage in the Netherlands and ensures that monthly payments remain affordable. It is possible to repay faster if the customer's financial situation allows, without any early repayment penalties or additional costs. After 30 years, or earlier with accelerated repayment, the customer is the full owner of the property and no further payments to DAR are required.

title: Market-based fee structure
content: The usage fee charged by DAR Hypotheken is market-based. This means the total costs are comparable to what is common on the Dutch mortgage market. The fee is calculated based on DAR's share in the property and a percentage comparable to the market interest rate, but it is explicitly not interest. The fee decreases as the customer repays more and DAR's share becomes smaller. DAR offers various terms for the fixed-rate period of the fee, allowing customers to choose between more certainty or flexibility in their monthly costs.

title: No interest - usage fee instead
content: A fundamental difference between the DAR Mortgage and a traditional mortgage is that no interest (riba) is charged. In Islamic finance, interest is prohibited. Instead, the customer pays a usage fee to DAR for the part of the property that is still owned by DAR. This fee is based on the fact that DAR is actually a co-owner of the property and the customer pays a compensation for the use of this property. This is an essentially different legal and financial construct from a loan with interest. The structure has been reviewed and approved by independent Sharia scholars.

title: Sharia compliance and certification
content: The DAR Mortgage is fully Sharia-compliant. The product has been reviewed and certified by Noor Shariah Solutions, an independent and internationally recognized Sharia advisory firm. Noor Shariah Solutions has confirmed that the structure of the DAR Mortgage complies with the principles of Islamic finance, including the prohibition of interest (riba), the prohibition of excessive uncertainty (gharar), and the requirement of a real underlying value (asset-backed). DAR Hypotheken is continuously monitored to ensure the product continues to meet Sharia guidelines. Customers can therefore trust that their home financing is halal.

title: Who is the DAR Mortgage for?
content: The DAR Mortgage is accessible to everyone, regardless of religion or background. While the product is designed according to Islamic finance principles, it is an excellent alternative for anyone looking for a transparent and fair way of home financing without interest. The DAR Mortgage is suitable for first-time buyers, those moving to a new home, and people who want to refinance their current mortgage to a halal alternative. Both individuals and couples can apply. The standard acceptance criteria are comparable to those of other mortgage providers in the Netherlands, including income assessment and property valuation.

title: The application process
content: Applying for a DAR Mortgage follows several steps. First, you can request an introductory consultation through the website or contact us by phone. Next, a personal advisory meeting is scheduled where possibilities are discussed based on the financial situation. After the advisory meeting, a mortgage application is submitted. DAR assesses the application based on income, the value of the property, and other relevant factors. Upon approval, the completion takes place at the notary, after which the customer and DAR become joint owners of the property. The entire process is comparable to a regular mortgage application and is supervised by experienced mortgage advisors.

title: Contact and company information
content: DAR Hypotheken is based in Amsterdam. You can contact us via email at info@darhypotheken.nl or by phone at 020-210 1656. The DAR Hypotheken website offers comprehensive information about the product and possibilities. Through the website, you can also request an introductory consultation or schedule an appointment with a mortgage advisor. DAR Hypotheken is registered with the Chamber of Commerce and operates under the supervision of the Dutch Financial Markets Authority (AFM). The DAR team consists of experienced financial services professionals who are passionate about making halal home financing accessible in the Netherlands.
